title: grindelia stricta subsp. blakei
intro: Grindelia stricta subsp. blakei, also known as gumweed, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it a popular ingredient in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Grindelia stricta subsp. blakei include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
